text.title: rag-eval-pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-eval-pipeline enforces.

rule.statement: Run every question in the test set; never sample for the baseline-establishing eval.
rule.rationale: Sampling for baselines understates the variance and misses tail failures.

rule.statement: Compute retrieval metrics and generation metrics as separate stages and report both columns; never collapse to one quality score.
rule.rationale: Collapsing hides where the failure is (retrieval vs generation).

rule.statement: Aggregate per-query rows into a single JSON report with means, p95, hit rate, and per-bucket breakdowns.
rule.rationale: Single-file artefact is the unit of comparison across runs.

rule.statement: A pipeline change PR cannot merge until the new eval report is attached and a human acknowledges it.
rule.rationale: Automation alone cannot catch novel failure modes; human review is the safety net.

rule.statement: Snapshot the pipeline version, judge model, embedding model, vector index hash, and test set hash into the report header.
rule.rationale: Without these, the report is not reproducible.

rule.statement: Skip when ground truth is unavailable or test set is below 20 questions.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
